title: SCD41 NDIR CO₂ Sensing Module
design_question: Which CO₂ technology provides ±50 ppm accuracy without user recalibration?
selected_approach: Sensirion SCD41 NDIR sensor (I²C, ±40 ppm, ABC self-calibration). On-chip temperature and pressure compensation.
rationale: NDIR is a primary technique (Beer-Lambert law) with <2 %/year drift. ABC algorithm uses fresh-air reference automatically—no user action needed.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Heavy cross-sensitivity to multiple gases; weeks of burn-in; not selective for CO₂—rejected.
Acceptable accuracy but lacks on-chip RH compensation.

verification_notes: FACT: 'NDIR CO₂ Sensor — Non-Dispersive Infrared' confirms Beer-Lambert principle and ABC baseline correction.
